label: world | lie | time
sentence: In the time before memory, when the dense German forests were shrouded in endless night, the world lay in total darkness.

label: rabe | grow | ∅
sentence: The Rabe, a cunning black bird who had existed since the beginning of days, grew weary of stumbling blindly through the dark woods and mist.

label: rabe | discover | cottage
sentence: One evening, the Rabe discovered the small cottage of a solitary old man who lived deep in the Schwarzwald with his young daughter.

label: rabe | learn | observation
sentence: Through quiet observation and craft, the Rabe learned that the old man possessed a priceless treasure: all the light in the world, locked away inside a tiny wooden box, nested within box after box, hidden beneath thick woolen cloths embroidered with ancient runes.

label: rabe | vow | ∅
sentence: Determined, the Rabe vowed to bring light back to the shadowed forests.

label: he | ponder | ∅
sentence: He pondered long and devised a clever plan.

label: rabe | transform | himself
sentence: When the old man’s daughter came to draw water from the cold, dark river, the Rabe transformed himself into a single, sharp spruce needle and drifted gently down the current, slipping into her wooden bucket.

label: needle | slide | throat
sentence: As she drank, the needle slid down her throat and settled deep within her warmth.

label: rabe | change | ∅
sentence: There, the Rabe changed once more, becoming a small human child.

label: time | pass | silence
sentence: Time passed slowly in the dim silence of her womb, until finally the Rabe was born into the world as a peculiar boy.

label: heart | soften | ∅
sentence: Though marked by his strange looks, the old man’s heart softened, and he loved this child as a grandson.

label: he | warn | ∅
sentence: Yet he fiercely warned that no one should ever touch the sacred box of light.

label: child | beg | this
sentence: Despite this, the Rabe child begged to hold the glowing treasure even for a moment.

label: man | relent | ∅
sentence: At last, the old man relented.

label: he | lift | lid
sentence: He lifted the small box’s lid and revealed a radiant, glowing orb that pulsed like a tiny sun.

label: he | throw | it
sentence: He threw it gently toward his grandson.

label: boy | transform | bird
sentence: As the glowing orb approached, the boy transformed rapidly into a great black bird, its vast wings unfurling like storm clouds over the forest canopy, its sharp beak wide and ready.

label: rabe | catch | orb
sentence: With a triumphant croak, the Rabe caught the orb in his beak.

label: he | burst | flap
sentence: With mighty flaps, he burst through the cottage’s thatched roof, flying out through the smoke hole into the night sky, his prize shining like a beacon in his beak.

label: light | return | day
sentence: Thus, from that day forward, the light returned to brighten the forests and fields, chasing away the long shadows, and filling the world with day once more.
